verses.text: I write that you sin not; but if any sin, 'we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ,' the 'propitiation for our sins.' Our advocate. Christ pleads our cause and covers our sin.
I write that you sin not; but if any sin, 'we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ,' the 'propitiation for our sins.' Our advocate. Christ pleads our cause and covers our sin.
We know we know Him 'if we keep his commandments'; in the obedient 'is the love of God perfected.' Proof of knowing. Obedience shows we truly know Him.
We know we know Him 'if we keep his commandments'; in the obedient 'is the love of God perfected.' Proof of knowing. Obedience shows we truly know Him.
We know we know Him 'if we keep his commandments'; in the obedient 'is the love of God perfected.' Proof of knowing. Obedience shows we truly know Him.
He that abides in Him ought 'so to walk, even as he walked'; the new commandment is true, for darkness passes and light shines. Walk like Christ. To abide is to follow His steps.
He that abides in Him ought 'so to walk, even as he walked'; the new commandment is true, for darkness passes and light shines. Walk like Christ. To abide is to follow His steps.
He that abides in Him ought 'so to walk, even as he walked'; the new commandment is true, for darkness passes and light shines. Walk like Christ. To abide is to follow His steps.
He that hates his brother 'is in darkness even until now'; he that loves abides in light. Love or dark. Hatred blinds; love walks in the light.
He that hates his brother 'is in darkness even until now'; he that loves abides in light. Love or dark. Hatred blinds; love walks in the light.
He that hates his brother 'is in darkness even until now'; he that loves abides in light. Love or dark. Hatred blinds; love walks in the light.
He writes to little children, young men, and fathers, because their sins are forgiven and they have overcome the wicked one. All ages. Each stage of faith has its assurance.
He writes to little children, young men, and fathers, because their sins are forgiven and they have overcome the wicked one. All ages. Each stage of faith has its assurance.
He writes to little children, young men, and fathers, because their sins are forgiven and they have overcome the wicked one. All ages. Each stage of faith has its assurance.
'Love not the world'; its lust and pride pass away, 'but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever.' Fading world. The world dies; the obedient endure.
'Love not the world'; its lust and pride pass away, 'but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever.' Fading world. The world dies; the obedient endure.
'Love not the world'; its lust and pride pass away, 'but he that doeth the will of God abideth for ever.' Fading world. The world dies; the obedient endure.
It is 'the last time'; antichrists have come, who 'went out from us, but they were not of us.' False sign. Departers prove they never belonged.
It is 'the last time'; antichrists have come, who 'went out from us, but they were not of us.' False sign. Departers prove they never belonged.
You have 'an unction from the Holy One' and know the truth; he that denies the Son has not the Father. Anointed knowing. The Spirit teaches you to discern the truth.
You have 'an unction from the Holy One' and know the truth; he that denies the Son has not the Father. Anointed knowing. The Spirit teaches you to discern the truth.
You have 'an unction from the Holy One' and know the truth; he that denies the Son has not the Father. Anointed knowing. The Spirit teaches you to discern the truth.
You have 'an unction from the Holy One' and know the truth; he that denies the Son has not the Father. Anointed knowing. The Spirit teaches you to discern the truth.
Let what you heard 'from the beginning' abide in you, and He has promised 'eternal life.' Abide. Holding the first truth secures the promise.
Let what you heard 'from the beginning' abide in you, and He has promised 'eternal life.' Abide. Holding the first truth secures the promise.
The anointing you received 'abideth in you,' and teaches you all things; abide in Him. Inner teacher. The Spirit confirms and guides the believer.
The anointing you received 'abideth in you,' and teaches you all things; abide in Him. Inner teacher. The Spirit confirms and guides the believer.
Abide in Him, that at His coming we have confidence, not shame; every one that does righteousness 'is born of him.' Be ready. Righteous living marks the new birth.
Abide in Him, that at His coming we have confidence, not shame; every one that does righteousness 'is born of him.' Be ready. Righteous living marks the new birth.
